author: @HHoudinido
text:
Hello Robert!
I think it's all about the positively charged atoms in the air around. For example near a  waterfall or in a forest it will have the worst performance because of the electron density. How we go upper and upper, we can reach more positive ions. 
We need the best air for this (maybe few km tall from the ground) and something what can speed up the ionisation.
Do you have idea how possible increase it? In addition to radiactive stuff. :)
... and sorry for my english.

author: @VenkateshksDr
text: In essence the SWAG plates convert any type of EM waves to electricity AC or DC doesn't matter now at this early stage.  Probably they can also convert the minute Van Der Waals forces to useful energy for which you'll have to try it out with plain water but at a considerable velocity.  Then hey presto you have a hydro electric generator on the lines of plasma MHD generator but on a cooler scale.  Its understandable with ionic liquids but with plain water the physics should be real interesting. :)

author: @llonajuice7207
text:
I think Tesla was wrong about his radiant energy which is probably why they pulled the funding for his tower changes for sending wireless power.
Also something a lot of people don't know is atmospheric electricity increases with height at about 30 volts per foot up to 300,000 volts at the top;     perhaps if you lacquered the plate you might get more voltage because you're insulating it from the leakage to earth;  also it would be better on dry days etc.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: +Llona Juice interesting ideas - have you run an experiment or two?

replies.author: @llonajuice7207
replies.text: +Robert Murray-Smith  no,  but i'm researching for one;   for 5 years on and off I've been pondering the Moray device,  and to be honest I've never had any theories that were credible enough to try out - it had me completely and utterly stumped!  then just recently I stumbled on an experiment which was although unrelated and I had a flash of inspiration,  and I thought to myself could this be it!  - the only problem it uses radioactive materials,  HV and custom tubes which are outside my comfort zone of digital electronics and aren't something you can pick up at digikey  ;-)

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: +Llona Juice an experiment - however small is always a good one mate - it goes a lot further with me than theories

replies.author: @llonajuice7207
replies.text:
+Robert Murray-Smith well theories are cheap to experiment with - for a tight Yorkshireman like me! ;-)
Have you tried a Roentgen tube over your swag coated plate, as per Tesla's Radiant energy patent?

replies.author: @llonajuice7207
replies.text: +Llona Juice  The issue I have with Tesla's radiant energy collector was he states in his patent that the energy collected is proportional to the area of the plate;  but Moray's device was different, as he knew exactly how to tap into the energy;    I think he stumbled on it by accident as a weird anomaly he found whilst he was playing around with tubes.

replies.author: @maxgatrell2056
replies.text: Thankyou for responding. I have a theory that if a positive and negative charge that came directly from a radiant energy source were to be applied to a glass containing graphite oxide, then the less stable elements would disperse (possibly downwards) and GNRs would be left on top.

author: @ArkiverUnifiedEnergy
text:
You got me thinking. There are similarities of the SWAG plate to that of a leaf in nature. The carbon, grain of annealing, and copper as stem. It's likely you created a man made leaf minus the chlorophyll, which is really cool.

No need to risk falling off roof. Attaching a piece of pvc pipe to the ladder with Swag on it and running it up should give a result.

There is something I think I need to share about the Tesla mystery to help advance our search for free energy. Tesla made many great contributions to science for which we are all grateful for his work. In my research though I have found that several of his patents are variations of others patents. The radiant energy patents are actually 11 years after the original inventor. It was invented by Mark W. Dewey in 1889. There is a lot of useful information in these patents that I think are key to making this a power source.
414,943 http://www.google.com/patents/US414943
418,912 http://www.google.com/patents/US418912
I believe the Tesla patents have a two pole capacitor because he couldn't duplicate the three pole cap of Dewey for voltage conversion.

My resources for testing are extremely limited right now, but I'd like to contribute my ideas and help with research when I can.

replies.author: @ArkiverUnifiedEnergy
replies.text: http://www.google.com/patents/US414943

replies.text: you have interesting ideas mate - thanks
